title: Innodata Q1 Earnings & Revenues Top Estimates, 2026 Sales View Up
text: Innodata Inc. exceeded analyst expectations in its first-quarter 2026 earnings, delivering adjusted earnings per share of 42 cents substantially surpassing the consensus estimate of 13 cents. The company's revenue climbed to $90.1 million, representing 54.4% year-over-year growth and beating the expected $76 million mark. The performance was propelled by heightened demand for artificial intelligence-related data services. The company expanded existing customer programs and secured new engagements supporting sophisticated AI workflows, particularly around training and post-training needs, as well as evaluation and deployment support for advanced AI systems. Operationally, Innodata demonstrated improved profitability despite cost increases. Adjusted gross margin expanded 400 basis points to 47%, while operating cash flow surged to $37.3 million from $10.8 million year-over-year. The company bolstered its cash position to $117.4 million, up from $82.2 million at year-end 2025. Management raised its 2026 revenue growth guidance to approximately 40% or greater, up from the prior 35% projection. A significant new Big Tech partnership is projected to generate roughly $51 million in 2026 revenues, positioning this customer as the company's second-largest account.
